title: Concern is Not that China Will Stop Buying Imported Iron Ore Cargoes
section_path: Concern is Not that China Will Stop Buying Imported Iron Ore Cargoes
section_path_text: Concern is Not that China Will Stop Buying Imported Iron Ore Cargoes
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
Dry bulk spot rates were mixed last week, with capesize and panamax rates declining. There has remained a lot to be bullish about in the dry bulk market, though, and structurally we are still just primarily concerned for the capesize market that iron ore exporters could pull back on sales due to the recent plummet in iron ore prices. Image reference: Chart 1.jpg | assets/2024-04-03_concern-is-not-that-china-will-stop-buying-imported-iron-ore-cargoes_img_chart1_4a7863cc790b.jpg
As we have been stressing in Commodore Research's Weekly Dry Bulk Reports, China's iron ore port stockpile capacity is about 230 million tons.
